summary.intent: Implement GitHub issue #279: a framework-agnostic core state machine for the ScrollArea component in the ars-ui Rust UI library, covering state transitions, events, effects, and API surface without any platform-specific code.
summary.outcome: Full implementation delivered in PR #699 across ~6 hours: state machine with 4 states (Idle/Hovering/ScrollActive/ThumbDragging), complete event/effect/context/props/messages/API types, 8-part Part enum, 64 inline unit and snapshot tests, proptest, and spec-conformance harness. PR went through 6 Codex review passes fixing 21 findings; pass 6 fixes committed but merge verdict still pending.

repo: Named-effect intent pattern: agnostic core emits PendingEffect::named(EffectName::AutoHide, no_cleanup()) as a marker; the adapter owns the timer and sends Event::HideTimeout back. Core never calls platform APIs. Same pattern as tooltip/rating_group.
context_changed in SendResult is true whenever any apply closure runs — NOT a value-diff. Tests asserting a no-op must not check context_changed == false after a handler that runs a closure.
Canonical RTL support uses ars_collections::normalize_scroll_left_rtl + RtlScrollMode re-exported at crate root. Local sign-heuristic reimplementations break at raw==0 for positive mode.
Delay props convention: plain Duration field named <action>_delay (no _ms suffix), with DEFAULT_<ACTION>_DELAY: Duration = Duration::from_millis(N) constant. Matches tooltip/hover_card/avatar.
update_visibility(active: bool) must receive an explicit flag to correctly gate Hover/Scroll visibility state; callers compute active from the resulting state.
Orientation gating belongs in agnostic core: ScrollOrientation::allows_x()/allows_y() gate visibility flags in update_visibility. Leaving it to adapters is a footgun.
SyncProps event + on_props_changed (like collapsible) required for any prop-backed context fields — omitting means controlled prop changes never sync after mount.
ThumbDragging must be included in is_session_active — resize/enter/visibility checks must not hide scrollbars mid-drag.
Corner data-ars-visible is true only when both X and Y scrollbars are visible. Scrollbar track uses data-ars-orientation not aria-orientation (decorative, not interactive).
clamp_offsets helper centralizes per-axis clamping to [0, content - viewport] and must be called after Scroll, Resize, and SyncProps events.
Snapshot budget is enforced by a CI gate (xci snapshot-count); orphan check requires --features i18n to cover i18n-gated fixtures.
Spec compile-snippets gate compiles fenced code blocks; adapter sketch snippets need rust,no_check.
Always-mode visibility invariant in proptest must exclude HideTimeout — it hides unconditionally regardless of mode.

Pre-existing clippy::unnecessary_option_map_or_else in pagination/mod.rs caused by floating @stable moving to 1.96.0 blocks any clippy run on ars-components; requires a mechanical one-liner fix as a side-task.

code.finding: ThumbDragMove clamps the offset at 0 (lower bound) but not at the upper bound (track_size - thumb_size), allowing the thumb to be dragged past the end of the track.
context_only closures require explicit |ctx: &mut Context| type annotation — the compiler cannot infer the receiver type in this macro context, causing repeated build-fix iterations.
context_changed is set whenever an apply closure runs, not based on value comparison. Tests must account for this when reasoning about no-op transitions.
normalize_scroll_left_rtl and RtlScrollMode are defined here and re-exported at crate root — the canonical RTL normalization implementation.
Pre-existing map_or_else clippy error triggered by compiler version bump to 1.96.0; blocks all clippy runs on ars-components until fixed.
Anatomy/spec-conformance tests for layout components including ScrollArea live here.
Layout proptests with orientation-gated invariants (e.g., Always-mode invariant must exclude HideTimeout events) live here.

workflow: Codex review loop: post @codex review, poll for thumbs-up or threads, fix each finding with TDD (failing test → fix → spec sync → xci-fast → commit → push → reply inline → resolve → re-trigger). Never merge without thumbs-up.
cargo xci-fast (10-step gate) is mandatory before every push.
Post-implementation audit runs in three phases: Phase 1 (spec/impl drift section-by-section), Phase 2 (adjacent surfaces, 2 clean rounds), Phase 3 (llvm-cov branch coverage).
Apply snapshot-reviewed label to any PR adding .snap files; re-verify and reapply if snapshots change in later commits.
Git worktrees are used for feature branches.

When Codex challenges an architectural boundary, investigate canonical components (tooltip, rating_group) before deciding — if the boundary is correct, reply 'by design' with rationale and resolve the thread.

friction: Rust closure type inference inside context_only required explicit |ctx: &mut Context| annotation on every closure, causing multiple build-fix iterations.
Messages type needed a manual PartialEq derive that was absent from the initial spec example.
insta --accept was blocked by pre-existing i18n-gated highlight snapshots (unreferenced without the feature flag), requiring manual promote and review.
Background snapshot jobs raced, leaving stale .pending files.
Pre-existing pagination clippy error blocked all clippy runs on ars-components.
Push after the pass-2 commit silently did not complete, requiring an explicit follow-up push command.
6 Codex review passes and 21 total findings — the ThumbDragging/visibility/timer interaction space was underestimated, leading to cascading edge cases across passes.
User had to correct hide_delay_ms: u32 to hide_delay: Duration — the initial spec example used a plain integer and the error was not caught in the initial audit.
open_items: Codex pass 6 @codex review re-triggered but verdict is pending — PR #699 is not yet merged.
Adapter components (Leptos + Dioxus) for ScrollArea are out of scope for this task — AutoHide timer scheduling, pointer-capture wiring, and RTL mode detection land in separate adapter tasks.
Axis { X, Y } in scroll_area vs ars-dom's Axis { Horizontal, Vertical } naming inconsistency — cross-crate alignment deferred.
End-to-end tests are not applicable until adapter components exist.
